Tuesday, May 26, 2026 Daily Digest

Google's "Ask Advisor" Agent Goes Cross-Platform, KPMG Deploys Claude to 276K, HSBC Says "Don't Fight AI"

Today's top stories in AI + enterprise tech

1

Google Marketing Live: "Ask Advisor" Agent Spans Entire Stack

Google unveiled its most ambitious AI play yet: a unified agent called "Ask Advisor" built on Gemini that connects Google Ads, Analytics, Merchant Center, and Marketing Platform. Think of it as an always-on strategic partner that connects the dots across your entire marketing operation. The message is clear: the era of siloed marketing tools is over.

Read on Google Blog →
2

KPMG Integrates Claude Across 276,000 Workforce

Anthropic announced a strategic alliance with KPMG to deploy Claude across its entire global workforce of 276,000+ professionals. This isn't a pilot — it's full-scale enterprise AI transformation. The Big 4 are moving fast, and KPMG just made the boldest bet yet on agentic AI for professional services.

Read on Anthropic →
3

HSBC CEO to Staff: "Don't Fight AI" as Banks Begin Job Cuts

Reuters reports HSBC's CEO is telling employees not to resist AI as the bank begins workforce reductions. The message is spreading across financial services: AI isn't coming — it's here, and the adjustment period is now. For enterprise leaders, the question isn't whether AI will impact your function, but how fast you can adapt.

Read on Reuters →
4

The SEO-to-GEO Shift: Optimizing for AI Discovery

The AI Insider profiles AirOps CEO Mark Halliday, who's helping define "Generative Engine Optimization" (GEO) — the emerging discipline of structuring content for AI visibility. As buyers increasingly start journeys in ChatGPT and AI search, teams that don't adapt their content for machine consumption risk becoming invisible.

Read on The AI Insider →
5

Meta's $145B CapEx Bet Meets 8,000 Job Cuts

AI Weekly's latest newsletter captures the tension defining 2026: Meta announced $145B in AI infrastructure spending while simultaneously cutting 8,000 jobs. This isn't contradiction — it's the new normal. Companies are investing massively in AI capabilities while restructuring the human workforce around them.

Read on AI Weekly →

💡 My Take

Read this one: The Google Marketing Live announcements. "Ask Advisor" isn't just another AI feature — it's Google declaring that the future of marketing is a unified agent layer sitting above your entire stack. When Google connects Ads, Analytics, Merchant Center, and Marketing Platform through a single AI interface, they're not just improving workflow — they're redefining what "marketing operations" means.
